slugfesterFeature.summary: Slugfester carries a parallel reference entry for this bias, giving readers another angle on how uncertainty and unclear options distort judgment.

slugfesterFeature.summary: Slugfester’s version is a useful companion when you want a second framing of how status, prestige, and deference bend what gets accepted as true.

slugfesterFeature.title: See base-rate neglect on Slugfester
slugfesterFeature.summary: This companion entry adds another teaching lens on what happens when vivid specifics crowd out the broader statistical backdrop.

slugfesterFeature.title: See belief bias on Slugfester
slugfesterFeature.summary: Slugfester’s reference page offers a complementary way to teach the gap between whether a conclusion feels right and whether the reasoning actually holds.

slugfesterFeature.title: See confirmation bias on Slugfester
slugfesterFeature.summary: This cross-link introduces a parallel reference page on Slugfester, useful for readers who want another presentation of selective evidence-seeking in action.

slugfesterFeature.title: See scope neglect on Slugfester
slugfesterFeature.summary: Slugfester’s take makes a good companion when teaching how quantity, scale, and total impact can disappear behind a simpler emotional response.

slugfesterFeature.summary: This linked entry gives a second presentation of how a statement can feel personally true because it fits hopes, impressions, or prior beliefs.
